response:
a retired schoolteacher in rural russia who respects the orthodox church decides to create a unique geometric design for a new church icon using principles of sacred geometry the design is based on two main components a golden rectangle and a circle inscribed within it

the golden rectangle is defined such that the ratio of its length to its width is the golden ratio phi equals 1 plus root 5 over 2 if the width of the rectangle is 2 meters the area of the rectangle is 2 times 2 equals 4 square meters the exact length of its diagonal can be calculated using the pythagorean theorem where the length is phi times the width the diagonal is sqrt(phi squared plus 1) times the width the exact length of the diagonal is sqrt(phi squared plus 1) times 2

the radius of the inscribed circle is half the width of the rectangle which is 1 meter the exact area of the region inside the rectangle but outside the circle is the area of the rectangle minus the area of the circle the area of the rectangle is 4 square meters the area of the circle is pi times 1 squared which is pi the exact area of the region inside the rectangle but outside the circle is 4 minus pi
